questions.question: The correct lens formula is:
questions.explanation: The lens formula is $\frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{u} + \frac{1}{v}$, identical in form to the mirror formula.

questions.question: The magnification produced by a lens is given by:
questions.explanation: For lenses, $m = v/u$. Unlike mirrors, there is no negative sign in the formula; the sign of $v$ determines orientation.

questions.question: An object is placed $30\text{ cm}$ from a convex lens of focal length $20\text{ cm}$. What is the image distance?
An object is placed $15\text{ cm}$ from a convex lens. The image forms $45\text{ cm}$ on the opposite side. What is the magnification?
questions.explanation: $m = v/u = 45/15 = 3$. The image is magnified 3 times.

questions.question: An object placed $25\text{ cm}$ from a convex lens produces a real image $50\text{ cm}$ from the lens. What is the focal length?
An object is placed $24\text{ cm}$ from a concave lens of focal length $12\text{ cm}$. What is the image distance?
options: $8\text{ cm}$ on the same side as the object
$12\text{ cm}$ on the same side as the object

questions.question: A $4.0\text{ cm}$ tall object is placed $20\text{ cm}$ from a convex lens. The image forms $60\text{ cm}$ on the opposite side. What is the image height?
A positive magnification for a lens indicates the image is:
questions.explanation: For lenses, positive $m$ means the image is upright relative to the object. This typically occurs with virtual images.

questions.question: A convex lens of focal length $10\text{ cm}$ forms a real image $40\text{ cm}$ from the lens. What is the object distance?
A convex lens of focal length $15\text{ cm}$ produces a real image with magnification $3$. What is the object distance?
In the lens formula, a negative value of $v$ indicates:
options: A real image on the opposite side of the lens
A virtual image on the same side as the object
A real image on the same side as the object
questions.explanation: Negative $v$ means the image is virtual and forms on the same side of the lens as the object.

questions.question: The power of a lens is measured in:
questions.explanation: Lens power $P = 1/f$ (with $f$ in meters) and is measured in diopters (D). A lens with $f = 0.5\text{ m}$ has a power of $2\text{ D}$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the lens formula and magnification calculations!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review sign conventions for concave lenses and the meaning of positive vs. negative $v$.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on $\frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{u} + \frac{1}{v}$ and $m = v/u$ for lenses.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: lens formula, magnification, and sign conventions for convex and concave lenses.
